summary: Validated the two new features (Daily Summary + CSV export). 13/13 backend pytest cases passed and frontend Settings page renders both buttons with correct disabled state plus a real CSV download.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~712 lines and starting to grow — recommend splitting routes/background_tasks/balance_helpers into separate modules before adding more features (file-size guideline: <700 lines).

_compute_daily_summary timestamp filter assumes trade.ts is stored as ISO string and uses lexicographic $gte/$lt comparison. This relies on consistent timezone-suffix formatting; any future change writing trades with a different ts format (e.g. naive datetime, epoch) will silently break aggregation. Consider storing ts as a real BSON datetime + indexing.

daily_summary_task fires when now_wib.hour==0 and minute<10 with a sleep of 30s. If the backend restarts shortly after 00:00 WIB after summary was sent, last_daily_summary_date is reset in memory, so the message could be sent twice. Consider persisting last_daily_summary_date in MongoDB.
/api/export-trades-csv loads all trades into memory (buf = StringIO) before streaming — fine for now but will become a memory issue once history grows; consider true async streaming.
seed_data_creation: Each aggregation test inserts TEST_<uuid>-prefixed trades into db.trades and deletes them in a finally block. test_csv_after_reset_has_only_header calls POST /api/reset-stats which wipes the trades collection — this matches the feature spec but DOES mutate global state, so do not run alongside any test that expects pre-existing trades.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite for iter5 lives at /app/backend/tests/test_daily_summary_and_csv.py. It avoids pytest-asyncio dependency by calling the live HTTP endpoint plus a synchronous pymongo client for seeding. The TestCsvAfterReset class deliberately calls /api/reset-stats — keep it last or in its own run if you add new tests that depend on existing trade data. The frontend btn-test-daily-summary is correctly disabled when has_telegram is false; to test the enabled path you need real Telegram creds saved via POST /api/settings.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration. Two implementation-time concerns flagged under critical_code_review_comments (timestamp filter relies on ISO-string ordering; daily_summary_task de-dup is in-memory only).
